title: XAIP v1 — Extended AI Prompt
description: A complete multi-turn AI prompt exchange. Covers the full conversation structure: a system turn built from atom refs (persona, constraints, format), parameterized user turns, tool definitions, and optional few-shot assistant examples.

id.description: URL-safe slug; unique within the XAIP catalog.

name.description: Human-readable name for this prompt exchange.

turns.description: Ordered sequence of turns in this exchange. A valid exchange must include at least one system or user turn.

turn.description: A single turn in the multi-turn exchange. The role determines which optional fields are applicable: atom_refs for system/user, tool_def for tool, slots for any parameterized turn.

role.description: The conversation role this turn occupies. 'system' establishes agent behavior via atom refs; 'user' provides the human message; 'tool' declares an available tool; 'assistant' provides a few-shot example response.

content.description: Static text content for this turn. For system/user turns may coexist with atom_refs (content is appended after assembled refs). For assistant turns this is the few-shot example text. Not used when role is 'tool' (use tool_def instead).

atom_refs.description: Ordered list of atom references assembled into this turn's content. Primarily used in system turns to compose persona + constraints + format. May also appear in user turns for reusable user-turn fragments.

tool_def.description: Tool definition for this turn. Required when role is 'tool'; not valid for other roles.

slots.description: Named parameters that callers substitute before the turn is sent. Slot names appear in content or atom_ref version strings as {{slot_name}}. Any role may declare slots.

atom_ref.description: A pinned reference to a single atom in the prompt-atoms catalog.

version.description: Exact semver of the referenced atom. Pinned to prevent silent behavior drift.

tool_def.description: Definition of a tool available to the model during this exchange. Mirrors the tool-call structure supported by major LLM providers.

name.description: Tool identifier sent to the model. Must be unique within the exchange's tool turns.

description.description: Natural-language description of what the tool does and when to call it. The model uses this to decide whether to invoke the tool.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema object describing the tool's input parameters. The renderer passes this to the provider API verbatim.

slots.description: Map of slot name to slot definition. Slot names must be valid identifiers (letters, digits, underscores). Values in content are referenced as {{slot_name}}.

slot_def.description: Declaration of a single slot (substitution parameter) within a turn.

type.description: Expected JSON type of the substituted value.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this slot represents and how it affects the turn.

required.description: Whether callers must supply this slot. Defaults to true; optional slots may be omitted, leaving their {{placeholder}} unresolved unless a default is provided.

default.description: Default value used when the slot is optional and no value is supplied. Type must match the slot's declared type.

metadata.description: Non-functional metadata for discovery, tooling, and documentation.

description.description: Long-form description of the exchange's purpose, intended context, and expected behavior.

tags.description: Free-form labels for search and filtering in the catalog.

vendors.description: LLM families this exchange is known to work well with. 'any' means vendor-agnostic. Inherits from the referenced atoms' own vendor declarations when absent.

use_cases.description: Short use-case labels (e.g., 'code-review', 'intent-classification', 'document-summarisation') describing where this exchange is intended to be applied.
